Go. wraiqus\* adj. 'id.', OFri. wrcik adj. 'id.'
ON reita w.v. 'id.', OHG reizen w.v. 'to vex, tease, tempt', G reizen w.v. 'id.' (GM). A causative-iterative formation to \*wrltan- (q.v.).

ON rei/Jr adj. 'angry', Far. rei/Jur adj. 'id.', OE wrii!/J adj. 'id.', OS wreth adj. 'wretched, hostile', OHG reid, reidi adj. 'curly, wavy' (ORV). An adjective derived from the strong verb \*wrlpan- - \*wrltan- (q.v.). Also cf. ON, Icel., Far. reioi, OSw. vrepe, Elfd. rwieoe m. 'anger' < \*wraipan-.

OE wrecca m. 'exile, stranger', OS wrekkio m. 'foreign warrior, stranger, exile', OHG reck(e)o m. 'fugitive, hero', G Recke m. 'champion' (ORV). A jan-stem directly derived from \*wrekan- (q.v.). The word probably originally meant 'avenger', i.e. one who ventures into unknown territory, whence both 'champion' and 'exile'. Also note OFr. garңun, Fr. garңon 'boy, servant', which is assumed to continue OLFra. \*wrakkio.

ON rindil-pvari m. 'id.', Ice!. rindill m. 'id.', Nw. dial. rennil m. 'wagtail', OE wrenna, werna m. 'wren', E wren, OS wrendo, wrendilo m. 'id.', OHG wrentilo m. 'id.' (GM). A word that is difficult to reconstruct because of the derivational irreconcilability of the attested forms: 1) ON rindill < \*wrendila-; 2) OS wrendilo, OHG wrentilo < \*wrandilan-; 3) OE wrenna < \*wrandjan-. No certain etymology. Unrelated to MDu. wranten w.v. 'to mope', but possibly connected to Ice!. rindill m. 'tail'. Elfd. rwainjugel m. 'brambling' does not belong here, but was derived from rwaina s.v. 'to wail' < \*wrlnan- (q.v.).

ON rangr adj. 'wry, crooked; wrong', Far. rangur adj. 'slanted, inside out, wrong', Elfd. rwaungg adj. 'contrary, obstinate', OE wrang n. 'wrong', E wrong, MDu. wranc adj. 'bitter, sharp, unfriendly, brave', Du. wrang adj. 'bitter' (ORV). An o-grade adjective derived from \*wringan- (q.v.).

OE wrencan w.v. 'id.', MHG renken w.v. 'id.', G renken w.v. 'id.' (ORV). One way of arriving at an etymology is to assume a causative formation related to an unattested verb \*wrinkan-, which in turn could be a nasal present to \*werkan- (q.v.). The verb is more likely to have been derived from the iterative \*wrunkon- < \*urr.;igh-neh₂-, however, for which cf. MDu. wronckelen 'to twist, squirm, wrap'. This iterative clearly belongs to the strong verb \*wringan- (q.v.).
OE wrcette f. 'id.', OHG rezza f. 'id.'
Go. wraton w.v. 'to travel', ON rata w.v. 'to travel; to meet with, find (the way)', MHG razzeln w.v. 'to turn'

Go. wrikan s.v. 'to persecute', ON reka s.v. 'to hunt, pursue', OE wrecan s.v. 'to drive; to punish', E to wreak, OFri. wreka s.v. 'to revenge', OS wrekan s.v. 'id.', Du. wreken s.v. 'id.', OHG rehhan s.v. 'id.', G riichen w.v. 'id.'

ON rak f. 'id.', OFri. wreke f. 'revenge', OS wraka f. 'id.', Du. wraak c. 'id.', OHG rah ha f. 'id.', G Rache f. 'id.' (DRY). A lengthened-grade formation derived from \*wrekan- (q.v.). Also cf. the vṛddhi-gerundive ON rcekr adj. 'abominable' < \*wreki- and Go. wrekei f. 'persecution'.

MDu. wrijghe, wrijch, wrijf m. 'id.', Du. wreef c. 'id.', OHG riho m. 'id.', G Reihen m. 'id.'
Nw. vrina s.v. 'to howl; to bare one's teeth', Elfd. rwaina s.v. 'to howl, wail'
OE wringan s.v. 'to wring, twist', E to wring, OS iit-wringan s.v. 'to squeeze out', Du. wringen s.v. 'to wring', OHG ringan s.v. 'to wring; to wrestle', G ringen s.v. 'id.'

Gutn. rind 'clubmoss', ME writhe/ 'wild lettuce(?)' (GM). Etymology uncertain. It is possible that both the formations Gutn. rind ( < \*wrinda-) and ME writhe/ ( < \*wrinpla-) were derived from a root \*wrinp- 'to wind'. This could technically be a nasal infIXed root belonging to the strong verb \*wripan- - \*writan- 'to twist' (q.v.).
OE writan s.v. 'to write; to carve', OFri. writa s.v. 'to write', WFri. write s.v. 'to tear; to ache, hurt', OS writan s.v. 'to tear; to write'
Go. writs m. 'id.', MDu. ret m. 'crack, fissure, lacuna', oHG riz m. 'scratching, line, letter', G Rifl m. 'crack, scratch' (ORV). An i-stem to the strong verb \*wrftan- ( q.v.).
OHG rizz6n, rezz6n w.v. 'id.', G ritzen w.v. 'id.'

ON rfoa s.v. 'to turn, ring, tie', OSw. vripa s.v. 'to ring', Elfd. rwaioa s.v. 'to turn, twist, ring', OE wrioen s.v. 'to twist, ring', E writhe s.v. 'id.', MDu. writen s.v. 'to twist, ring, wrap', OHG ridan s.v. 'to wind, turn', MHG riden s.v. 'to wind, wrap'

ON rot, p. rretr f. 'id.', Far. rot, pl. rږtur f. 'id.', OSw. rot f. 'id.', Elfd. ruot, pl. ryoter f. 'id.' (EUR?). A root noun usually reconstructed as PGm. \*wrot-. The Joss of the initial \*w is unexpected in East Norse, however. This means that the standard etymological connection with \*wurti- 'root' can only be maintained by assuming ad hoc deletion of\*w.
Go. wruggo f. 'snare, noose' (ORV). Also cf. MDu. wronc m. 'noose, wreath; twisting; anger', Du. wrong c. 'braid' < \*wrunga-. Derived from \*wringan- ( q.v.).
Go. wulan s.v. 'to boil, be hot'
Go. wulfs m. 'id.', ON u/fr m. 'id.', Far. ulvur m. 'id.', Elfd. ulv m. 'id.', OE wu/fm. 'id.', E wolf, OS wulf, wolfm. 'id.', Du. wolf c. 'id.', OHG wo/f m. 'id.', G Wo/fm. 'id.'
ONylgr,ylgja f. 'id.', Elfd.ylva f. 'id.', OHG wulpa f. 'id.', MHG wiilpe f. 'id.'

Ice!. 6lga 'to foam, swell, seethe' (also cf. 6lgra w.v. 'to ferment, boil'), Far. ulka w.v. 'to vomit, be sick', Nw. ulka w.v. 'to be nauseated, feel sick', G wulgern w.v. 'to be nauseated'
Go. wulla f. 'id.', ON ull f. 'id.', Far. ul/ f., OE wull f. 'id.', E wool, OFri. wol/e f. 'id.', Du. wol c. 'id.', OHG wolla f. 'id.', G Wolle f. 'id.'
